taxonomy: Tauschia stricklandii is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is a biennial or perennial herb that produces small white or pinkish flowers. Tauschia stricklandii is classified in the genus Tauschia, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Carum. The exact classification within the family Apiaceae is still being researched and refined by botanists.
